name: CoordinateInput
summary: Base class implementation of the coordinate input

params.name: bZCoord
params.doc: Z coordinate input is active: true/false

methods.summary: Add the geometry elements from the preview elements for the point and element search
methods.remarks: Add the geometry elements from the preview elements for the point and element search

methods.summary: Explicit cancel of the input function
methods.remarks: Explicit cancel of the input function

methods.summary: Enable the change of the x/y-focus after each input
methods.remarks: Enable the change of the x/y-focus after each input

params.name: bChangeXYFocus
params.doc: if true, the xy focus will be changed

methods.name: EnableCoordinateInput
methods.summary: Enable / disable the coordinate input This function can be used to enable or disable the coordinate input.
methods.remarks: Enable / disable the coordinate input This function can be used to enable or disable the coordinate input.

methods.name: EnableUndoStep
methods.summary: Enable disable the undo step
methods.remarks: Enable disable the undo step

methods.name: EnableZCoord
methods.summary: Enable the z-coordinate inside the coordinate dialog
methods.remarks: Enable the z-coordinate inside the coordinate dialog

params.name: bEnable
params.doc: Z coordinate inside the coordinate dialog is enabled: true/false

methods.summary: Get the associative view from the point

methods.name: GetCurrentPoint
methods.signature: GetCurrentPoint() -> CoordinateInputResult | GetCurrentPoint(startPnt: Point3D) -> CoordinateInputResult | GetCurrentPoint(startPnt: Point3D, bStartPnt: bool) -> CoordinateInputResult | GetCurrentPoint(bStartPnt: bool) -> CoordinateInputResult
methods.summary: Get and mark the current input point
methods.remarks: Get and mark the current input point

methods.name: GetInputAssocView
methods.summary: Get the associative view of the input point / element The function can be used after a call to GetInputPoint, SelectGeometryElement, SelectElement, ...
methods.remarks: Get the associative view of the input point / element The function can be used after a call to GetInputPoint, SelectGeometryElement, SelectElement, ...

returns.doc: Associative view of the input point / element, NULL if point is outside

methods.name: GetInputPoint
methods.signature: GetInputPoint( mouseMsg: int, pnt: Point2D, pMsgInfo: AddMsgInfo ) -> CoordinateInputResult | GetInputPoint( mouseMsg: int, pnt: Point2D, pMsgInfo: AddMsgInfo, bStartPnt: bool ) -> CoordinateInputResult | GetInputPoint( mouseMsg: int, pnt: Point2D, pMsgInfo: AddMsgInfo, startPnt: Point3D, bStartPnt: bool, ) -> CoordinateInputResult

methods.name: GetReferenceLine
methods.summary: Get the reference line, on which the identified point is located. This method works when eIdentificationMode is set to eIDENT_ARCH... and only with on architectural elements
methods.remarks: Get the reference line, on which the identified point is located. This method works when eIdentificationMode is set to eIDENT_ARCH... and only with on architectural elements

returns.doc: Reference line

methods.name: GetSelectedElement
methods.summary: Get the selected element The function can be used in case of eIdentMode = MODE_TEXTPOINT, SelectGeometryElement, SelectElement, ...
methods.remarks: Get the selected element The function can be used in case of eIdentMode = MODE_TEXTPOINT, SelectGeometryElement, SelectElement, ...

returns.doc: Selected element

methods.summary: Get the related associative view of the selected element
methods.remarks: Get the related associative view of the selected element

methods.name: GetSelectedElements
methods.summary: Get the selected elements The function can be used in case of eIdentMode = MODE_TEXTPOINT, SelectGeometryElement, SelectElement, ...
methods.remarks: Get the selected elements The function can be used in case of eIdentMode = MODE_TEXTPOINT, SelectGeometryElement, SelectElement, ...

returns.doc: Vector of elements with the same distance to the cursor

methods.name: GetSelectedGeometryElement

methods.remarks: Get the selected geometry element

methods.name: GetSelectedGeometryElements
methods.summary: Get the selected geometry element
methods.remarks: Get the selected geometry element

returns.doc: IGeometry elements of the selected geometry elements (with the same distance)

methods.name: SelectElement
methods.signature: SelectElement( mouseMsg: int, pnt: Point2D, pMsgInfo: AddMsgInfo, bHighlight: bool, bSelAlways: bool, bAllowCenter: bool, ) -> bool | SelectElement( mouseMsg: int, pnt: Point2D, pMsgInfo: AddMsgInfo, bHighlight: bool, bSelAlways: bool, bAllowCenter: bool, selectSetting: ElementSelectFilterSetting, ) -> bool
methods.summary: Perform element search Uses the filter set by the SetElementFilter
methods.remarks: Perform element search Uses the filter set by the SetElementFilter

params.name: bSelAlways
params.doc: Relevant when used in combination with GetInputPoint method. When set to False, the element search is terminated after the method GetInputPoint found a start/end/mid point of an element. When set to True, the element search will be performed anyway.

params.name: bAllowCenter
params.doc: Relevant when used in combination with GetInputPoint method. When bSelAlways is set to False and this option to True, the element search is performed after the method GetInputPoint found a mid point. When bSelAlways is set to True, this option becomes irrelevant.

returns.doc: True, when element is selected. False otherwise

methods.name: SelectGeometryElement
methods.signature: SelectGeometryElement( mouseMsg: int, pnt: Point2D, pMsgInfo: AddMsgInfo, bHighlightCompleteElement: bool = False, ) -> bool
methods.summary: Select a base geometry element. Use the filter set by SetGeometryElementFilter and SetGeometryFilter
methods.remarks: Select a base geometry element. Use the filter set by SetGeometryElementFilter and SetGeometryFilter

params.name: bHighlightCompleteElement
params.doc: true = highlight the complete element, false = highlight only the selected geometry part

returns.doc: Element was found: true false

methods.summary: Set the abscissa element
methods.remarks: Set the abscissa element

params.name: abscissaAssoMat
params.doc: Abscissa matrix of the associative view

methods.name: SetElementFilter
methods.summary: Set the element selection filter
methods.remarks: Set the element selection filter

methods.name: SetGeometryElementFilter
methods.summary: Set the geometry element selection filter
methods.remarks: Set the geometry element selection filter

methods.name: SetGeometryFilter
methods.summary: Set the geometry filter used for geometry search during point input (initialized with InitFirstPointInput or InitNextPointInput). This filter is used, when the identification mode (eIdentificationMode) is set to identify points and geometry elements, i.e. to eIDENT_POINT_ELEMENT...
methods.remarks: Set the geometry filter used for geometry search during point input (initialized with InitFirstPointInput or InitNextPointInput). This filter is used, when the identification mode (eIdentificationMode) is set to identify points and geometry elements, i.e. to eIDENT_POINT_ELEMENT...

methods.name: SetInputPlane
methods.summary: Set the input plane. The input point will be projected onto this plane along the plane's normal vector.
methods.remarks: Set the input plane. The input point will be projected onto this plane along the plane's normal vector.

methods.name: SetInputText
methods.summary: Set the input text
methods.remarks: Set the input text

params.name: text
params.doc: Request string as resource ID, str

methods.name: SetInputTextPrefix
methods.summary: Set the input text prefix
methods.remarks: Set the input text prefix

methods.name: SetProjectionBase0
methods.summary: Set the projection base to 0 during point input in floor plan view or side view When this option is set to True, then if a point is found and snapped during the input inside a floor plan view or a side view (front, rear, left or right), the resulting point will be projected on: When this option is set to False, then the resulting point is not projected onto those planes. Instead it is being snapped to the first visible point from the observer's perspective. Default value is: False
methods.remarks: Set the projection base to 0 during point input in floor plan view or side view When this option is set to True, then if a point is found and snapped during the input inside a floor plan view or a side view (front, rear, left or right), the resulting point will be projected on: When this option is set to False, then the resulting point is not projected onto those planes. Instead it is being snapped to the first visible point from the observer's perspective. Default value is: False

params.name: setProjectionBase0
params.doc: True, to set the projection base to 0
